title: KYMEntryScrape
description: Deterministic scrape-layer schema for a Know Your Meme entry. Scope: confirmed memes only. Every field here is directly liftable from the raw HTML with no interpretation. `required` lists the fields structurally guaranteed on essentially every confirmed-meme page (url/title/category/status/origin, all measured 100% on a 5,574-entry sample) — it validates well-formedness. Corpus-completeness requirements (year, region, entry_type, tags, about/origin/spread sections) are a SEPARATE, tunable gate (CorpusPolicy), not schema `required` — even a 99.7%-coverage field like tags legitimately has real confirmed memes without it, so thin entries are flagged and reported rather than rejected outright. Narrative sections are captured as faithful blocks (text/links/images); interpreted summaries belong to the downstream extraction schema, not here.

schema_version.description: Version of THIS scrape schema. Bump only when the scrape contract changes (kept separate from EXTRACTOR_VERSION).

url.description: Canonical entry URL on knowyourmeme.com. Primary key; also the join key back to the Mongo `doms` document.

category.description: Top-level entry kind, from the breadcrumb / URL namespace. Present on 100% of the dump.

status.description: Entry confirmation status from the details sidebar. Coerce anything unrecognised to 'unknown' rather than failing.

entry_type.description: Type slugs from details.type, normalised from the /types/<slug> URLs (e.g. 'image-macro', 'catchphrase'). Multiple per entry is normal.

year.description: Origin year from the details sidebar. Source is a string or null; coerce to int, leave null if absent/non-numeric. Do NOT require it (~11% are null).

origin.description: Origin platform/source label from the details sidebar (e.g. '4chan', 'I Can Has Cheezburger'). Raw label only — no interpretation. Required: present on 100% of confirmed memes.

region.description: Region/country labels from the details sidebar. Sparse — absent in the old dump; capture when the sidebar exposes it, else [].

aliases.description: Alternative names, from an 'Also Known As' field when present. Sparse.

tags.description: Flat list of tag strings from the entry footer. Deduplicate, preserve order. NOT schema-required (moved to CorpusPolicy.require_tags): 99.7% coverage on confirmed memes, but the missing 0.3% are legitimate entries that should be kept and flagged incomplete, not rejected.

badges.description: Explicit badges from the sidebar's 'Badges:' row (e.g. 'Sensitive'). This is the authoritative NSFW/content-warning signal, preferred over any URL-path inference — pages under /sensitive/ carry a 'Sensitive' badge here.

template_image_url.description: The entry's template/icon image (template_image_url in the dump; 100% present there).

og_image.description: Social card image (meta og:image). Provenance/thumbnail use.

parent.description: Parent entry URL if this is a sub-entry (covers the old 'parent_meme' intent, losslessly, as a link).

additional_references.description: The 'Additional References' sidebar box. In the dump this is a {name: url} map (present on 53.5%); flattened here to a list so ordering and duplicate names survive.

external_references.description: The numbered [n] citations from the References section, structured. Replaces the flat string list, which lost the URL/label split.

sections.description: The narrative body as an ordered list of blocks, one per heading. Order-preserving and collision-free (keying by heading text produced ~3000 junk keys in the old dump). Downstream indexes by `kind`.

meta.description: Raw og:/twitter: meta tags, kept verbatim for provenance and as an extraction fallback (og:description is a clean one-liner).

kym_last_updated.description: KYM's own last-updated timestamp (unix seconds), from last_update_source. Page-authored, not scrape time.

scraped_at.description: When this scrape ran (ISO 8601). Set by the scraper, not from the page.

heading.description: Raw heading text, normalised (trimmed, lowercased). Guard against embed-script blobs and cap length so Google-Trends JS never lands here as a heading.

kind.description: Best-effort canonical bucket for the heading. The long tail all maps to 'other' but keeps its raw `heading`.

level.description: Heading level (H2 vs H3) so subsection nesting is recoverable.

text.description: Paragraphs under this heading, in order. Strip embed scripts/trend-widget noise here.
